title: SEBI's Bold Strokes: Navigating the New Era of Buy-backs, AIFs, Mutual Funds, and Securities Transmission
content:
# SEBI's Bold Strokes: Navigating the New Era of Buy-backs, AIFs, Mutual Funds, and Securities Transmission

As a professional navigating the dynamic Indian financial landscape, one thing is certain: change is the only constant. And when SEBI, our market regulator, steps in with a fresh set of reforms, we know it's time to sit up and take notice. The decisions from the SEBI Board Meeting on June 19, 2026, are a testament to this proactive approach, ushering in significant shifts across buy-backs, Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs), Mutual Funds, and critically, the process of securities transmission. *Chalo*, let's decode these pivotal changes and understand their implications.

This isn't just about regulatory compliance; it's about reshaping market efficiency, easing operational burdens, and bolstering investor protection. So, what exactly has the Board approved, and how does it impact us and our clients?

## Buy-Backs: The Grand Re-Entry of the Open Market Route

Remember when open market buy-backs through stock exchanges were phased out? Many companies and market participants felt a key flexibility was lost. Well, good news! SEBI has decided to reintroduce the **open market share buyback route through stock exchanges**, effective from **August 1, 2026**. This move aims to provide greater flexibility in undertaking buy-backs, reduce procedural complexity, and strengthen investor protection.

Why the reintroduction? Primarily, it follows revisions in the taxation framework, where buy-back proceeds are now taxed as capital gains in the hands of shareholders, addressing previous concerns about uneven treatment and tax arbitrage.

Here's a quick look at the revised framework for open market buy-backs:

| Old Provision (Post 2025 Phase-out)           | New Provision (Effective Aug 1, 2026)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   The **SEBI (Buy-back of Securities) Regulations, 2018**, have been amended for this purpose.

Promoter participation in the open market buy-back will be barred, primarily to avoid any potential for tax arbitrage, ensuring that such buybacks are treated as normal trading transactions. The buy-back process will need to be completed within **66 working days** from its opening, with a mandate to deploy at least **40% of the earmarked funds** within the first half of the buy-back period (33 working days). This is an important step to ensure efficient utilization of funds and a timely conclusion of the process.

## AIFs: A Green Channel for Faster Fundraising with GARUDA

For Alternative Investment Funds, SEBI has introduced a significant ease-of-doing-business measure: the **Green-Channel: AIF Rollout Upon Document Acknowledgement (GARUDA) mechanism**. This framework is designed to drastically cut down the time it takes for eligible AIFs to commence fundraising.

Under GARUDA, eligible funds will now be able to start their fundraising activities within a mere **10 working days** of filing their placement memorandums, a substantial reduction from the earlier 30-day waiting period. For **angel funds**, the process is even faster, allowing immediate deployment by removing the requirement for merchant banker mandates.

This is a game-changer for the AIF industry, which relies on swift capital deployment. *Thoda faster ho jayega ab!* It will undoubtedly boost capital formation through AIFs and align with the broader objective of improving the ease of doing business in private market vehicles.

Beyond GARUDA, there are also discussions around AIFs being allowed to retain liquidation proceeds beyond their fund life to address unresolved liabilities and the introduction of an 'Inoperative Fund' category for schemes facing such issues, ensuring proper management during winding-up.

## Mutual Funds: Intraday Borrowings for Liquidity Management

The mutual fund industry has also received a practical reform concerning liquidity management. The SEBI Board has approved amendments to the **SEBI (Mutual Funds) Regulations, 2026**, to permit mutual funds to undertake **intraday borrowings**.

This facility is specifically designed to manage temporary liquidity mismatches that might arise during the trading day. Think about scenarios like bridging differences due to pay-in/pay-out settlement timings across asset classes, foreign exchange settlements, or making payments for mark-to-market (MTM) derivative positions.

However, SEBI has put crucial safeguards in place. The regulator has explicitly clarified that these intraday borrowings **shall not be used as a source of leverage**. Furthermore, it will be the responsibility of the Asset Management Companies (AMCs) to ensure that such borrowings are **repaid by the end of the day**. Any borrowing extending overnight will still need to comply with existing regulatory limits. This move provides essential operational flexibility without compromising the fundamental principle of responsible fund management.

## Securities Transmission: A Smoother Journey for Legal Heirs

Perhaps one of the most impactful reforms for the common investor and their families is the overhaul of the **securities transmission process**. Dealing with the transfer of assets after the demise of a loved one can be a cumbersome and emotionally taxing experience. SEBI's new framework aims to make this process significantly faster, simpler, and less burdensome for legal heirs and claimants.

Here are the key simplifications:

*   **Quick Transmission Processing (QTP):** A new category has been introduced for small-value claims. This means claims up to **₹10,000 for physical holdings** and up to **₹30,000 for dematerialised holdings** can now be processed with minimal documentation. *Chhote claims ke liye ab zyada paperwork nahi!*
*   **Doubled Limits for Simplified Documentation:** The thresholds for simplified documentation have been significantly enhanced. For physical holdings, the limit has been increased from ₹5 lakh to **₹10 lakh per listed company**. For dematerialised holdings, the limit has been raised from ₹15 lakh to **₹30 lakh per beneficial owner**. This means more families can bypass complex legal procedures for larger amounts.
*   **Removal of PAN Requirement:** The need to submit a Permanent Account Number (PAN) during transmission has been removed. SEBI rightly observed that PAN details are already available when demat accounts are opened, making additional submission redundant.
*   **Dispensing with Mandatory Probate of Will:** In a major relief, the mandatory requirement of probate of a will has been done away with, aligning the framework with recent amendments to succession laws. This can save significant time and legal costs.
*   **Combined Affidavit-cum-NOC:** Claimants will now be permitted to submit a combined affidavit-cum-no objection certificate (NOC) instead of separate documents, streamlining the process further.
*   **QR Code-Enabled Death Certificates:** To simplify verification, SEBI will now accept death certificates carrying QR codes, alongside original or attested copies. For foreign-issued death certificates, specific verification mechanisms through Indian bank branches and foreign banks with correspondent relationships have been outlined.

These comprehensive reforms are expected to reduce costs, ease procedural hardships, and facilitate easier and faster transmission of securities for claimants. *Badi rahat mili hai logon ko ismein.*

## Final Thoughts

The SEBI Board's decisions on June 19, 2026, are clearly aimed at enhancing the overall efficiency, transparency, and ease of doing business in the Indian securities market. From bringing back the popular open market buy-back route with necessary safeguards to fast-tracking AIF launches through GARUDA, and providing mutual funds with much-needed intraday liquidity options, the regulator is demonstrating its commitment to market development.

The most welcome changes, in my opinion, are in securities transmission. By simplifying a process that often causes immense stress during difficult times, SEBI has truly put the investor first. These reforms, *ekdum practical aur zaroori the*, are poised to foster greater confidence and participation in our capital markets. As professionals, it's incumbent upon us to understand these nuances thoroughly and guide our clients effectively through this evolving regulatory landscape.

## Read the Official Document
*   [Download the Official Notification/Circular here](https://www.sebi.gov.in/media-and-notifications/press-releases/jun-2026/key-decisions-taken-in-the-sebi-board-meeting-dated-19th-june-2026_88975.html)

*Published on: 22 June 2026*
